title: andropogon scrobiculatus
intro: Andropogon scrobiculatus, commonly known as black grass or benghal grass, is a type of perennial grass native to India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and is also used to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Andropogon scrobiculatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or oil, which is used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
